mnemonics: LISTen to your Tuples, Dictate your Sets - four key Python data structures
keyPoints: Lists are ordered, mutable sequences for storing collections of items
Tuples are ordered, immutable sequences often used for fixed data
Dictionaries are unordered collections of key-value pairs for fast lookups
Sets are unordered collections of unique elements for membership testing
commonMistakes: Trying to modify a tuple after creation (immutable)
Using a mutable object like a list as a dictionary key
Assuming sets or dictionaries maintain insertion order (only true in Python 3.7+)
